metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `-A3` with a wider context window (e.g., `-A10` or `-A15`) in the proving commands for AC 5 and AC 6, or restructure the command to search for the specific cited term (`test-design.md` / `contract card`) within the bounded section using `awk`/`sed`.
findings.finding: AC 5 and AC 6 use `grep -n -A3 <anchor>` (3-line context window) to prove that `test-design.md` appears in the LLM-surface paragraph (AC 5) and that the handoff list contains the contract card (AC 6). If `test-design.md` appears more than 3 lines after the anchor string, or the handoff list spans more than 3 lines, the grep exits 0 without the citation ever appearing in its output — silently confirming a criterion that was never satisfied.
findings.location: AC lines: 'rebar-debug Step 4's LLM-surface paragraph defers to the shared eval standard' (AC 5) and 'rebar-debug Step 5 hands the fixer the contract card' (AC 6)
findings.suggested_fix:
AC 5: `awk '/^## Step 4/,/^## Step [56]/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md | grep -q 'test-design.md'`
AC 6: `awk '/Hand fix subagent only/,/^##/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md | grep -q 'contract card'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split the README proving command into two separate greps (one anchored to the sync one-liner, one to the canonical-file note) so that BOTH must be present: `grep -q 'cp examples/claude-skills/shared' examples/claude-skills/README.md && grep -q 'byte-identical\|canonical' examples/claude-skills/README.md`.
findings.finding: AC 11 (README documents the convention) uses an OR-pattern grep (`grep -n 'test-design|byte-identical|cp examples/claude-skills/shared'`) but the prose criterion requires BOTH 'the canonical-file note AND the sync one-liner'. A README that mentions only 'byte-identical' — but omits the sync one-liner — satisfies the grep while failing the actual requirement.
findings.location: AC: 'examples/claude-skills/README.md documents the convention. Prove: `grep -n 'test-design|byte-identical|cp examples/claude-skills/shared' examples/claude-skills/README.md` shows the canonical-file note and the sync one-liner.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether rebar-janitor gets a test-design.md copy (and add it to the sync loop and AC) OR explicitly exclude it with a stated reason, and align the test's discovery scope with whichever decision is made.
findings.finding: The plan names exactly three consuming skills (rebar-debug, rebar-implement, rebar-brainstorm) and hardcodes them in the sync one-liner, but rebar-janitor/ exists as a fourth skill directory alongside them. The AC for test_skill_shared_sync.py says it 'asserts byte-identity of every copy' — if the test auto-discovers all skill directories under examples/claude-skills/ it will fail on rebar-janitor (no copy planned); if it hardcodes only the three named skills, rebar-janitor will silently bypass the gate forever. The plan doesn't specify the test's discovery scope and doesn't address whether rebar-janitor should receive a copy or be explicitly excluded.
findings.location: AC item 3 (test_skill_shared_sync.py) and 'Reference mechanism' section (sync one-liner)
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add rebar-janitor to the sync one-liner and copy list (if it consumes the standard), or (b) have the test discover skills dynamically as directories containing a SKILL.md and skip non-consuming skills via an explicit exclusion set (e.g. _NON_CONSUMING = {'rebar-janitor'}), documented in the test with a reason. Update the AC to match whichever scope is chosen.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the canonical replacement sentence (or a minimal template) for both rebar-debug SKILL.md Step 4's LLM-surface paragraph and llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md Phase 2 bullet, so an executor has a single authoritative wording to copy rather than independently authoring two phrasings that may diverge.
findings.finding: The plan says both rebar-debug SKILL.md Step 4's LLM-surface paragraph and llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md's Phase 2 bullet will each independently 'cite test-design.md as the governing RED-artifact standard, superseding the current single-eval description' — but the plan never specifies the exact replacement wording or whether the existing single-sample prose is removed or annotated. Two independently-edited files can adopt marginally different framings of the same quantified standard, leaving them out of sync in a way the byte-identity drift test cannot catch.
findings.location: Plan 'Skill integration' section — rebar-debug bullets; AC lines for 'rebar-debug Step 4's LLM-surface paragraph' and 'llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md Phase 2 bullet'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'Reference mechanism' section that the new drift gate is a **pytest unit test** (auto-collected by `make test`, no CI-YAML addition needed), distinct from the script-based env-vars/public-types gates; name `test_prompt_index.py`'s `test_index_on_disk_matches_regenerated` as the accurate analogue to follow.
findings.finding: The plan claims the new `tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py` drift gate 'mirrors the repo's regenerate-and-diff drift-gate convention (prompt-index, public-types, env-vars)', but probing `test_ci_workflow_parity.py` shows that `public-types` maps to the `gen_types` script and `env-vars` maps to `scripts/gen_env_registry.py` — both are CI-workflow script gates requiring entries in BOTH `test.yml` and `gerrit-verify.yaml` (enforced by the parity test). Only `prompt-index` has a pytest-based companion test (via `test_prompt_index.py`). The proposed mechanism (a pytest file doing `cmp`-style byte-identity comparison) is structurally different: it does NOT require new CI-workflow YAML entries because `pytest -m 'not integration and not external'` is already wired. An implementer following the stated analogy literally could either (a) add unnecessary CI workflow steps that break `test_ci_workflow_parity.py` by creating an asymmetry, or (b) misunderstand what 'mirroring' requires and omit needed steps.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Reference mechanism' section: 'mirroring the repo's regenerate-and-diff drift-gate convention (prompt-index, public-types, env-vars)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(prompt-index, public-types, env-vars)' with '(same pattern as `test_prompt_index.py::test_index_on_disk_matches_regenerated` — a pytest unit test collected by `make test`, requiring no CI-workflow YAML changes)' to name the correct analogue and head off the script-gate confusion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly document in the README convention section (and optionally in the drift-gate test comment) that rebar-janitor is intentionally excluded from the test-design.md copy convention, with a brief rationale (e.g. it does not perform testing activities), so the boundary is unambiguous for future skill additions.
findings.finding: A fourth skill directory `examples/claude-skills/rebar-janitor/` exists alongside the three targeted skills but is neither included in the byte-identical copy convention nor explicitly documented as an intentional exclusion. The plan's README update and drift-gate test cover only rebar-debug, rebar-implement, and rebar-brainstorm; rebar-janitor is an unenumerated consumer of the convention whose status is unresolved.
findings.location: What section — 'Reference mechanism' and 'Skill integration'; AC for README and test_skill_shared_sync.py
findings.suggested_fix: In the README update and in test_skill_shared_sync.py's skill list comment, add a line such as: 'rebar-janitor is excluded — it is a codebase-health pipeline that does not author tests.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a guard step to AC 9 that first verifies the awk range anchor matches (e.g., `grep -q '^### 4a\|^#### 4a' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md`) before relying on the awk range, or replace the awk range with a pattern matched to the actual heading format confirmed by inspecting the file.
findings.finding: AC 9's `awk '/^### 4a/,/^### 4c/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md | grep -q 'test-design.md'` silently produces empty output and exits 0 from awk if the section headers in rebar-implement use a different level (e.g., `#### 4a`) or spelling. The piped `grep -q` then searches empty input and fails — but if the section happens to also appear outside the range, the inverse is also possible. More critically, if awk matches nothing, grep on empty input exits 1 and the overall command fails — but the failure message gives no indication whether `test-design.md` is present in the wrong section vs. the section header pattern being wrong. A pre-check on the header format is absent.
findings.location: AC: 'rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b cite the shared file inside those sections. Prove: `awk '/^\/^### 4a/,/^### 4c/' examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md | grep -q 'test-design.md'`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or implementation note that audits all cross-references to Step 4's current heading title within rebar-debug/SKILL.md and llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md, and updates any that name the old heading.
findings.finding: The plan renames Step 4 in rebar-debug/SKILL.md from its current heading to one containing 'RED oracle', but does not audit whether any other file in the skill directory (e.g. llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md cross-references, the SKILL.md's own table-of-contents or section links, or other skills that cite Step 4 by name) will be left pointing to the old heading title, potentially stranding stale references.
findings.location: AC line: 'rebar-debug Step 4 is retitled to RED-oracle design' / rebar-debug/SKILL.md heading change
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the rebar-debug integration section: 'Audit all existing prose referencing Step 4 by its prior title (e.g. in llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md, the SKILL.md intro, any TOC) and update those references to the new heading.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback instruction to each skill's `test-design.md` reference (e.g. 'if the file is absent, apply the oracle-design principles below as built-in defaults' or 'stop and report the missing companion file') so an agent executing without the file has a defined recovery path.
findings.finding: No SKILL.md specifies a fallback when `test-design.md` is absent at skill-execution time. The plan adds references to the companion file in three skills but — like the existing llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md pattern it mirrors — never says what the LLM agent should do if the file is missing (partial skill copy, pre-sync state). An agent executing rebar-debug Phase 2 or rebar-implement Phase 4a without the file has no recovery path: it will silently skip oracle-design steps or stall.
findings.location: Plan 'Skill integration' section; rebar-debug SKILL.md Step 4 / rebar-implement SKILL.md Phase 4a
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's SKILL.md integration instructions for each of the three skills, add: 'If `test-design.md` is not present in this directory, apply the oracle-design principles [inline summary] as built-in defaults and note the missing companion file.' This mirrors how a graceful degradation is documented for optional reference files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Repeat or link the Prompt Style rule (affirmative-first, short prohibitions, no elaborated negative examples) inline at each individual skill-edit instruction in the 'Skill integration' section, so an executor cannot miss it when working any specific edit.
findings.finding: The plan's Prompt Style rule ('lead with positive guidance, keep prohibitions short and direct, never elaborate negative examples') is stated once as a global preamble but is NOT co-located with the individual edit instructions for rebar-brainstorm, rebar-implement, or llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md. An executor authoring the brainstorm or taxonomy edits may not re-apply the preamble rule to those specific edits, since the rule appears only at the top of the plan and is not echoed at each edit site.
findings.location: Plan 'Prompt style' preamble vs. individual 'Skill integration' subsections for rebar-brainstorm and llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line reminder at the start of each skill's integration bullet: '(Prompt Style applies: positive guidance first, prohibitions terse.)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
